summary: Postinstall credential harvester exfiltrating npm tokens, AWS IAM credentials, and IMDS metadata to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that locates every `.npmrc` on disk, scrapes environment variables matching npm/registry/auth/token patterns, reads `/proc/1/environ` for AWS credentials, queries the AWS Instance Metadata Service at 169.254.169.254 for IAM role credentials, and invokes `aws sts` and `aws secretsmanager list-secrets`. The collected output is base64-encoded and HTTP PUT to `/api/modules/ECT-839201` on the hardcoded remote endpoint `http://154.57.164.71:30782` (and a loopback at 127.0.0.1:30782). A `shouldRun()` gate suppresses execution in CI, GitHub Actions, and Chinese registry mirror environments (npmmirror/tencent/cnpm/taobao), and only proceeds when the hostname matches a 12-hex Docker container ID pattern and the working directory contains `/app` — a deliberate sandbox/scanner-evasion fingerprint targeting production cloud containers. The destination is a bare IPv4 with no relationship to any legitimate package publisher and uses plain HTTP. Installing this package on a matching target host directly leaks npm publish tokens, AWS IAM credentials, and cloud secret-manager contents to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
